value.body:
## Context

The repository-root `KBAR_SPEC.md` (v0.1.0, 2026-07-03) defines a global KBar command/search surface and the reusable search contract that powers it. The work spans `@cycle/contracts`, `@cycle/database`, `@cycle/usecases`, `@cycle/api`, `@cycle/ui`, and `@cycle/desktop`.

Target repository: cycle://repository/repo_16371

## Outcome

Deliver an accessible global KBar opened by `Meta+K` or `Control+K`. It must combine renderer-owned commands, routes, settings, and recents with entity/resource results from a schema-backed `GET /v1/search` endpoint that searches all registered repositories by default.

`KBAR_SPEC.md` is the normative implementation specification for behavior, package ownership, safety, observability, failure handling, and validation.

## Acceptance criteria

- Add shared search query, context, resource-result, warning, pagination, and output schemas in `@cycle/contracts`.
- Add database search capabilities and a named search use case (for example `ResourceSearch`) without introducing UI or HTTP concerns into lower-level packages.
- Expose authenticated `GET /v1/search` from `@cycle/api`, searching all registered repositories by default and supporting the filters, pagination, warnings, error mapping, stable ranking, and partial-result behavior defined in the spec.
- Backend results contain entities/resources only; UI commands, routes, settings, and dialog openers remain renderer-local.
- Exclude deleted tickets and exclude archived tickets unless explicitly requested.
- Add presentation-only, accessible KBar components to `@cycle/ui`, including empty, loading, error, disabled, confirmation, long-content, and narrow-viewport Storybook states.
- Add the desktop KBar controller, local registries, deterministic result merging/ranking, stale-request handling, action execution, and versioned local recents/frequency storage.
- `Meta+K` and `Control+K` open KBar globally, including from editable fields; focus moves into the dialog and is restored appropriately.
- Empty queries immediately show local actions/settings/recents without waiting for the backend. Non-empty queries debounce `/v1/search`, preserve matching local actions, ignore stale responses, and keep local results usable on backend failure.
- Wire create ticket, create initiative where supported, add repository, settings, pages, repositories, tickets, and saved views to the existing dialogs, mutations, and canonical routes.
- Every action declares side-effect metadata; destructive/external actions follow the confirmation rules in the spec and execute only registered behavior.
- Add the contract, API, desktop, UI, accessibility, Storybook, and package-boundary coverage listed in the spec’s test matrix.
- Structured diagnostics do not log raw search query text, secrets, credentials, or unbounded metadata.

## Risks

- Cross-package ownership can drift; preserve the boundaries in the root and package specifications.
- Global shortcut handling must coexist with the current shortcut provider without duplicate dispatch or editor interference.
- Cross-repository failures must degrade to partial results and warnings where possible.
- Recents must remain renderer-local, bounded, versioned, and resilient to malformed storage.

frontmatter.title: Implement global KBar and reusable cross-repository search

value.title: Implement global KBar and reusable cross-repository search
